rulings.Case Title: PETER M OWENS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners' Application For Enforcement Of Writ Of Mandate And Issuance Of Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2022 line 1. PETITIONERS' APPLICATION FOR ENFORCEMENT OF WRIT OF MANDATE AND ISSUANCE OF ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT is GRANTED. A hearing on order to show cause why respondent shall not be held in contempt for failure to comply with the writ is set for March 15, 2022. Any response shall be filed and served no later than February 17, 2022. Any reply shall be filed and served no later than March 3, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY SERVICES VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf To Deposition And Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANTS BRYANT FU AND CRYSTAL LEI'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS is granted. Tina Yan to appear for her deposition in person on a mutually agreed upon date at Jan Brown and Associates, 701 Battery Street Suite 3, San Francisco, CA 94111no later than February 25, 2022, at 9:30 a.m. If the parties cannot agree on a mutually agreed upon date, then the deposition will be conducted at Jan Brown and Associates, 701 Battery Street Suite 3, San Francisco, CA 94111 on February 12, 2022, at 9:30 a.m. Defendants' request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $660.00. Defendants have provided the receipt from the deposition officer showing that they incurred $600 for the deposition. Defendants cannot recover attorney fees as they are acting in pro per. CCP section 1992 is unavailable to the defendants for recovery. Ms. Yan should have been served via notice of deposition since she is an officer/owner of Plaintiff Legal Recovery Services. Sanctions to be paid to defendants no later than January 31, 2022. Deposition can be video taped and/or audio taped if notice is given pursuant to the Code of Civil Procedure. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
